branch_decision.A_charge_two: set the physical fixed charge to k=2; obtains the triplet but abandons the macroscopic Q=10^6 thin-wall benchmark
branch_decision.B_separate_level_two_internal_sector: retain the macroscopic Q-ball charge but introduce an independent quantized level-two Wess--Zumino/Schwinger-boson sector
branch_decision.forbidden_shortcut: identify Q=10^6 with k=2 or infer O(2) merely from CP1

checks.name: normalized doublet defines a rank-one Hermitian projector
projector is invariant under the common U(1) phase
Pauli Hopf vector lies on S2 and reconstructs the projector
flat C2 metric splits into Hopf-fiber plus Fubini--Study metric
Fubini--Study metric agrees on north and south charts
Hopf connection transforms as A to A+d alpha while horizontal norm is invariant
Hopf curvature has first Chern number one
polar decomposition of the canonical doublet kinetic term is exact

checks.detail: global kinetic minus fiber=0.1057699414076649, local quotient=0.1057699414076649
checks.name: auxiliary local gauge field removes only the Hopf-fiber square
direct Routh transform equals the monopole-coupled CP1 expression
Legendre transform gives the minimally coupled monopole Hamiltonian
fixed-Q profile energy retains nonnegative orientation kinetic energy

checks.detail: k>0 regular-level gradient norm=2.828427; mu^-1(0) radius=0.0
checks.name: C2 moment-map quotient is CP1 only at positive level; k=0 is a point

checks.detail: dL/d(hbar*c)=k-N=0.300000; delta L/(hbar*alpha_dot)=2.000000; reduced flux level=+2
checks.name: corrected Branch-B symplectic sign imposes N=k and a positive level-k endpoint phase
Sym^0(C2) has k+1 states and the correct Cartan weights
Sym^1(C2) has k+1 states and the correct Cartan weights
Sym^2(C2) has k+1 states and the correct Cartan weights
Sym^3(C2) has k+1 states and the correct Cartan weights
Sym^4(C2) has k+1 states and the correct Cartan weights
Sym^5(C2) has k+1 states and the correct Cartan weights
Sym^6(C2) has k+1 states and the correct Cartan weights
k=2 Schwinger sector is the spin-one triplet
second-order k=2 rotor has a three-state LLL but infinitely many higher levels
monopole-rotor spectrum depends on |k| and is invariant under flux reversal
prior Q-ball angular frequency is Q divided by collective inertia
macroscopic Q=10^6 sector is not the k=2 triplet
three-dimensional size dilation has E2~lambda, E4~lambda^-1, E0~lambda^3
positive E2 plus E0 cannot have a finite-size Derrick stationary point
an E4 stabilizer can balance E2 at finite size
TeX contains the corrected action signs, charge domain, and signed-spectrum formulas

first_principles_chain: flat positive Hermitian metric on one complex doublet
normalization or a dynamically frozen radial mode
Hopf quotient and induced Fubini--Study metric

logical_result.fixed_global_charge: proved for the collective ansatz: Routh reduction gives a monopole-coupled T*CP1 rotor, not by itself H0(CP1,O(k))
logical_result.global_U1: refuted as a pointwise CP1 quotient: the local Hopf-fiber phase remains physical
logical_result.holomorphic_sector: conditional on first-order reduction or a controlled LLL/Kahler polarization: for k>=0, H_k=H0(CP1,O(k)), dim=k+1; for k<0 the conjugate anti-holomorphic sector has dim=|k|+1
logical_result.local_U1: proved under one nonzero charge-one complex doublet, fixed norm, and an auxiliary local U(1): S3/U(1)=CP1 with the induced FS action
logical_result.route_E_O2: not derived: k=2, the LLL gate, and the identification with T_CP1 and three chiral families remain separate assumptions

nonderived_inputs: why the microscopic order parameter is exactly one complex doublet
why the radial mode is frozen and what fixes its scale
why the relevant prequantum level is k=2
why the second-order rotor may be projected to its LLL
why the resulting spin-one triplet is a family carrier
how a stable four-dimensional bubble survives radial, gauge, and portal fluctuations
